# Brain — Meta-Index

> The brain is forge's persistent memory. This is the navigation hub: from here, drill into a category index → a theme page → the raw sources behind it.

**Status:** Generated by `forge brain index --write`. The brain holds **64 forge-level theme pages**, **37 project-level theme pages** (across 7 sub-wikis), **45 raw sources** (`_raw/docs/`, `_raw/web/`, `_raw/v1-wiki/`, `_raw/cycles/`, `_raw/projects/`), a structural index at [`graphify-out/`](./graphify-out/) (built by `safishamsi/graphify`; canonical `graph.json` committed per [CONTRACTS.md C20-C22](../docs/planning/2026-05-20-refinement/CONTRACTS.md)), and a benchmark question set. See [`docs/seeding-plan.md`](../docs/seeding-plan.md) and [`log.md`](./log.md).

## How to use this wiki

1. Start here. Pick a category or project below.
2. Open the category index — it lists theme pages with one-line descriptions.
3. Open a theme page — it summarises the topic in 15-40 lines and links to raw sources.
4. Follow raw links into [`_raw/`](./_raw/) when the theme page isn't enough.
5. For keyword search across raw: `grep -r '<term>' brain/_raw/`.

## Forge-system knowledge

- [Patterns](./forge/patterns.md) — proven approaches that work.
- [Antipatterns](./forge/antipatterns.md) — proven approaches that don't.
- [Decisions](./forge/decisions.md) — ADRs and the reasoning behind durable choices.
- [Operations](./forge/operations.md) — how to run / monitor / recover the system.
- [Reference](./forge/reference.md) — system overviews, profiles, comparisons, glossaries.

## Per-project sub-wikis

Each project has a `profile.md` (who / what / taste / hard-constraints) and a `themes/` directory with project-specific patterns, antipatterns, and decisions.

- [GitWeave](./projects/GitWeave/profile.md) — A single "control" repository that configures and weaves together a GitHub organisation using in-repo modules, overlays, and provider-native tooling.
- [env-optimiser](./projects/env-optimiser/profile.md) — Local-first workflow analysis and optimisation for WSL2 developers.
- [healarr](./projects/healarr/profile.md) — A self-healing agent for Plex/*arr media stacks.
- [simplarr](./projects/simplarr/profile.md) — Setup-and-configuration tooling for Plex/*arr media stacks (Sonarr, Radarr, Prowlarr, Overseerr, qBittorrent, etc.).
- [slugifier](./projects/slugifier/profile.md) — A shared canonical URL-safe slugifier package for a content pipeline.
- [terraform-provider-betterado](./projects/terraform-provider-betterado/profile.md) — A GitHub fork of `microsoft/terraform-provider-azuredevops` ("better ADO
- [trafficGame](./projects/trafficGame/profile.md) — A traffic simulation game with campaign mode.

## Conventions

- **Three layers**: `_raw/` (immutable raw sources) → `forge/themes/` and `projects/<name>/themes/` (15-40-line theme pages indexing the raw layer) → category index pages and this INDEX (pure navigation).
- **No long summaries** — many small theme pages > few large summaries (Karpathy).
- **Theme page format** — see [`forge/themes/README.md`](./forge/themes/README.md).
- **Lint rules** — see [`LINT.md`](./LINT.md).
- **Operations log** — append significant operations to [`log.md`](./log.md).

## Maintenance

- `forge brain index --write` — regenerate this file from the filesystem.
- `forge brain lint` — run structural integrity checks; flags orphans, malformed frontmatter, broken citations, contamination, oversized themes, and stale graph (9 checks total).
- `brain-query` / `brain-ingest` skills — query the brain / ingest new sources. **`brain-query` consults `graphify-out/graph.json` first** (structural questions via `graphify query` / `graphify path` / `graphify explain`) and falls back to keyword scan over themes (C20 dual-index).
- `brain-graph` skill — maintains the `graphify-out/` structural index. Run `graphify update .` after content changes (no API cost; tree-sitter local). Post-commit hooks rebuild automatically on operator machines.

## All themes (wikilink hub)

A wikilink-style hub block listing every theme — gives the graph a
high-degree connector node so LLM-backed semantic extraction (C20-C22)
can route cross-cluster relationships through INDEX. Maintained by
`scripts/brain-index-hub.ts`.
